content.problemStatement:
Chef gave you a sequence of integers a1,a2,‚Ä¶,aNa_1, a_2, \dots, a_Na1‚Äč,a2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,aN‚Äč and 333 integers KKK, mmm and xxx. Let's define the following functions:
G(i1,‚Ä¶,iK)=gcd(ai1,‚Ä¶,aiK)G(i_1, \ldots, i_K) = \mathrm{gcd}\left(a_{i_1}, \dots, a_{i_K}\right)G(i1‚Äč,‚Ä¶,iK‚Äč)=gcd(ai1‚Äč‚Äč,‚Ä¶,aiK‚Äč‚Äč)
Sx(i1,‚Ä¶,iK)=ai1x+ai2x+‚Ä¶+aiKxS_x(i_1, \ldots, i_K) = a_{i_1}^x + a_{i_2}^x + \ldots + a_{i_K}^xSx‚Äč(i1‚Äč,‚Ä¶,iK‚Äč)=ai1‚Äčx‚Äč+ai2‚Äčx‚Äč+‚Ä¶+aiK‚Äčx‚Äč
Pm(i1,‚Ä¶,iK)=(ai1‚čÖai2‚čÖ‚Ä¶‚čÖaiK)mP_m(i_1, \ldots, i_K) = \left(a_{i_1} \cdot a_{i_2} \cdot \ldots \cdot a_{i_K}\right)^mPm‚Äč(i1‚Äč,‚Ä¶,iK‚Äč)=(ai1‚Äč‚Äč‚čÖai2‚Äč‚Äč‚čÖ‚Ä¶‚čÖaiK‚Äč‚Äč)m
W(T)=smallest‚ÄČ‚ÄČprime‚ÄČ‚ÄČdivisor‚ÄČ‚ÄČof‚ÄČ‚ÄČP1(T)W(T) = \mathrm{smallest\,\, prime\,\, divisor\,\, of\,\, } P_1(T)W(T)=smallestprimedivisorofP1‚Äč(T)

Chef wants you to compute the sum of G(T)‚čÖW(T)‚čÖSx(T)‚čÖPm(T)G(T) \cdot W(T) \cdot S_x(T) \cdot P_m(T)G(T)‚čÖW(T)‚čÖSx‚Äč(T)‚čÖPm‚Äč(T) over all KKK-tuples of indices T=(i1,i2,‚Ä¶,iK)T = (i_1, i_2, \ldots, i_K)T=(i1‚Äč,i2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,iK‚Äč) such that ij‚ąą{1,2,‚Ä¶,N}i_j \in \{1, 2, \ldots, N\}ij‚Äč‚ąą{1,2,‚Ä¶,N} for each valid jjj (there are NKN^KNK of these KKK-tuples). Since this sum could be enormous, compute it modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.
constraints: **Subtask #2 (20 points):** for each valid iii, aia_iai‚Äč is even
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains four space-separated integers NNN, KKK, mmm and xxx.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers a1,a2,‚Ä¶,aNa_1, a_2, \ldots, a_Na1‚Äč,a2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,aN‚Äč.
content.outputFormat: Print a single line containing one integer ‚ąĎTG(T)‚čÖW(T)‚čÖSx(T)‚čÖPm(T)\sum_T G(T) \cdot W(T) \cdot S_x(T) \cdot P_m(T)‚ąĎT‚ÄčG(T)‚čÖW(T)‚čÖSx‚Äč(T)‚čÖPm‚Äč(T) modulo 109+710^9+7109+7.
